command_construction_guide.overview: Detailed instructions for constructing Quantum Safe Explorer CLI commands based on project context, language, and scan type requirements.

component.name: Command Separator
component.purpose: Chain commands to execute in sequence

parameter.name: input_path
parameter.description: The directory containing the source code to scan

construction_logic.step: Check if user specified a custom path
If not specified, use current workspace directory
Ensure path is absolute or properly relative
Verify path exists before constructing command

parameter.name: language
parameter.description: File extension indicating the programming language

construction_logic.step: Map detected language to appropriate extension
If detection fails, ask user to specify

parameter.name: class_file_path
parameter.description: Path to compiled Java class files (bytecode)
parameter.required: Only for Java Cryptographic Analytics scan

construction_logic.step: Only include for Java projects with -da flag
Search for common Java build output directories
Check in order: target/classes, build/classes, out/production, bin
If found, use that path; otherwise use input_path
Include ONLY for Cryptographic Analytics scan
NEVER include for API Discovery scan

algorithm.name: java_cryptographic_analytics
algorithm.description: Build command for Java Cryptographic Analytics scan

steps.step: Determine class file path: - Search for target/classes, build/classes, out/production, bin - If found, use that path - Otherwise, use same as input path

algorithm.name: java_api_discovery
algorithm.description: Build command for Java API Discovery scan

algorithm.name: non_java_api_discovery
algorithm.description: Build command for non-Java API Discovery scan

validation_checklist.check: -i parameter is present with valid path
-l parameter is present with valid extension
For Java Cryptographic Analytics: -cf and -da are present
For API Discovery: -da is NOT present
Command uses && to chain cd and execution

pattern.name: java_full_analysis
pattern.description: Complete quantum readiness for Java (both scans)

error_prevention.rule: Never include -da flag for API Discovery scans
Never include -cf flag for non-Java languages
Always verify input path exists before executing
Always use absolute path for QSE installation
For Java Cryptographic Analytics, always include both -cf and -da

path_resolution.guideline: When determining the input path (-i): 1. If user specifies a path, use it exactly as provided 2. If no path specified, use current workspace directory 3. Convert relative paths to absolute when possible 4. Verify path exists using list_files before constructing command

When determining class file path (-cf) for Java: 1. Use list_files to check for build output directories 2. Search in order: target/classes, build/classes, out/production, bin 3. If multiple found, prefer Maven (target) over Gradle (build) 4. If none found, use input path as fallback 5. Always use absolute paths for clarity

execution_notes.note: The command changes directory to QSE installation before executing, so the working directory during scan is /usr/local/bin/qse-cli-artifacts, not the project directory. This is why absolute paths are preferred.
For Java projects requiring quantum readiness assessment, you must execute TWO separate commands in sequence: 1. Cryptographic Analytics (with -da) 2. API Discovery (without -da)
The QSE tool generates its own reports and output files. Do not attempt to parse or summarize the output - simply confirm execution completed.
